stackRefinements.ui: React 18 SPA built with Vite 5; TypeScript (.tsx) alongside plain ESM (.mjs) utility modules; tests via node:test
stackRefinements.api: Express 4 HTTP server in TypeScript; plain ESM (.mjs) route helpers; tests via node:test
stackRefinements.db: Prisma 5 data layer targeting PostgreSQL (datasource in prisma/schema.prisma; models Asset, Tag); TypeScript client wrapper plus .mjs helpers; tests via node:test
stackRefinements.shared: Cross-cutting library: zod 3 validation schemas (tag-schema.ts) plus dependency-free ESM utilities (slugify.mjs); consumed via npm workspaces ("shared": "*")

ordering.rationale: shared is depended on by both apps (ui and api declare "shared": "*"), so contract/schema changes there must land first. db owns the Prisma schema and is a provider by role even though no package currently consumes it. api and ui are leaf consumers; ui last since it sits at the presentation edge.

gaps: db is not declared as a dependency by any other workspace package — the expected api -> db edge is absent, so db is currently an island; its provider position in dispatch ordering is inferred from role, not from a manifest edge.
ui and api declare a dependency on shared in package.json but no source file actually imports from it — declared edge is unexercised in code.
No tsconfig.json anywhere despite .ts/.tsx sources, and all build scripts are echo stubs — the TypeScript toolchain wiring cannot be verified.

No internal edge into ui from anywhere (expected: ui is a pure consumer), and api does not call db or expose data routes beyond /health, so the runtime data flow ui -> api -> db is implied by layer roles only, not by imports.
